definition: An entity that bears responsibility for activities and can act on behalf of itself or others.
notes: Umbrella class; subclasses are BFO-aligned. Aligns with prov:Agent.

synonyms: software agent, AI assistant, LLM agent
definition: An agent that is a software system capable of autonomous decision-making and tool use.
notes: Aligns with prov:SoftwareAgent. Information content entity.

definition: An agent that is a human person who interacts with AI systems.

synonyms: child agent, delegated agent, spawned agent
definition: An AI agent that was spawned by another agent to perform delegated work.
notes: Inherits GDC from AIAgent. Has spawnedBy link to parent.

definition: A role that an agent plays in the context of a conversation or activity.
notes: Aligns with prov:Role. Closed set of individuals via owl:oneOf.

synonyms: tool schema, tool descriptor, tool specification
definition: A generically dependent continuant that specifies the interface, capabilities, and constraints of an available tool.
notes: Describes tool name and JSON schema; does not model implementation.

synonyms: tool call, tool use, tool execution
definition: A process in which an agent requests execution of a tool by providing inputs and receiving outputs.
notes: Subclass of Action. N-ary pattern with input/output/status.

definition: A process consisting of a coherent thread of dialogue between participants that may span multiple sessions.
A process that is a bounded period of agent-user interaction within a conversation.
notes: Temporal part of a Conversation. Has status (Active/Ended/Interrupted).

definition: A process that is a single contribution by one participant in a conversation.
notes: Temporal part of a Session. Has turn index for ordering.

definition: A generically dependent continuant that is the textual or structured content of a conversational turn.
notes: Information content distinct from the Turn (process) that produced it.

definition: A process in which an agent's context window is compressed by summarizing prior turns.
notes: Subclass of Event. Produces a summary (MemoryItem).

definition: A generically dependent continuant that is an information artifact persisted in an agent's memory system.
notes: Aligns with prov:Entity. Uses ODP-4 (Information Realization).

synonyms: memory layer, memory level, storage tier
definition: A generically dependent continuant that defines a layer in the agent's memory architecture with specific storage and access characteristics.

synonyms: context window, short-term memory, in-context memory
definition: A memory tier that holds information currently active in the agent's context window.
notes: Maps to LLM context window. Most volatile tier.

synonyms: experience memory, event log, recall memory
definition: A memory tier that stores time-situated experiences and interaction histories.

synonyms: fact memory, knowledge base, long-term knowledge
definition: A memory tier that stores generalized facts, knowledge, and beliefs extracted from experience.
notes: Contains Claims. Letta: archival storage for facts.

definition: A memory tier that stores learned action policies, skills, and behavioral patterns.
notes: System prompts and tool-use policies. Least volatile tier.

definition: A memory item that records a temporally bounded cluster of related events.
notes: Subclass of MemoryItem only (not Event). See bfo-alignment.md for rationale.

definition: A memory item that represents an agent-held proposition with associated confidence, evidence, and provenance metadata.
A process that acts on memory items to encode, retrieve, consolidate, or remove them.
A memory operation that writes new information into a memory tier.
A memory operation that reads information from a memory tier.
A memory operation that transforms episodic memories into semantic memories.
A memory operation that removes or decays memory items from a memory tier.
notes: Includes passive decay and active deletion.

definition: A process that is an occurrence situated in time within the agent system.
An event that is intentionally caused by an agent to affect the environment or internal state.
notes: Subclass of Event. Has performedBy link to Agent.

definition: A generically dependent continuant that specifies a desired state an agent works toward.
notes: BDI goal concept. Has status and pursuedBy agent.

definition: A generically dependent continuant that specifies an ordered sequence of intended actions to achieve a goal.
notes: Aligns with prov:Plan. Has tasks as parts.

definition: A generically dependent continuant that is a concrete actionable work item within a plan with status and dependencies.
notes: Component of a Plan. Has blockedBy dependencies.

definition: A generically dependent continuant that specifies rules governing what actions an agent is permitted or forbidden to perform.
A generically dependent continuant that specifies a non-negotiable behavioral limit on an agent.
notes: Stronger than PermissionPolicy; cannot be overridden.

definition: A process in which memory items are identified and deleted to comply with privacy requirements.
notes: Subclass of Event. Records who requested and when.

definition: A class whose individuals represent the current state of an entity.
A status applicable to sessions indicating whether the session is active, ended, or interrupted.
A status applicable to tasks indicating progress from pending through completion.
A status indicating whether a tool invocation complied with applicable permission policies.
Status value: an entity is currently active.
Status value: a session has ended normally.
Status value: a session ended abnormally.
Status value: a task has not yet started.
Status value: a task is currently being worked on.
Status value: a task or process has finished successfully.
Status value: a task cannot proceed because of unresolved dependencies.
Compliance status: a tool invocation followed all applicable policies.
Compliance status: a tool invocation violated one or more policies.
The role of the AI assistant in a conversation.
The role of the human user in a conversation.
A claim type indicating the claim records a user preference.
notes: Used as a value for claimType property.

definition: A sensitivity level indicating the memory item contains personal data.
notes: Used as a value for hasSensitivityLevel.

definition: An agent that is a formal body or group of people.
notes: Aligns with prov:Organization. Subclass of Agent.

synonyms: system prompt, agent identity, configured persona
definition: A GDC that specifies an agent's configured identity behavior and system prompt.
An event in which an agent receives environmental feedback.
notes: Subclass of Event. Linked to Session via inSession.

definition: A memory operation that strengthens a memory item via repeated access.
notes: Subclass of MemoryOperation. Added to DisjointUnion.

synonyms: core memory block, key-value memory, Letta block
definition: A memory item that stores structured key-value data (Letta core memory pattern).
notes: Subclass of MemoryItem. Has hasBlockKey and hasBlockValue.

definition: A GDC representing an agent's commitment to execute a plan (BDI model).
notes: Linked to Goal via derivedFromGoal and to Agent via intendedBy.

definition: A status class whose individuals classify privacy sensitivity.
A GDC that records a data subject's consent for a specific purpose.
A GDC specifying how long memory items should be retained before deletion.
Sensitivity level: unrestricted data.
Sensitivity level: organization-internal data.
Sensitivity level: restricted personal data.
Sensitivity level: highly sensitive data requiring special controls.
A process in which an entity's status changes from one value to another.
notes: Subclass of Event. Reified transition with fromStatus/toStatus.

definition: A status transition in which a session's status changes.
A status transition in which a task's status changes.
An information artifact recording the fate of an individual item during a compaction event.
notes: N-ary reification linking event to item and fate.

definition: A status class whose individuals classify what happened to an item during compaction.
Item fate: content incorporated into summary.
Item fate: excluded from summary.
Item fate: partially represented in summary.
Item fate: moved to long-term storage.
